summary: Iteration 6 verifies the .gitignore-only fix. Confirmed `.env.example` is no longer git-ignored (negation rule on line 86 active, `git check-ignore` exits 1). Ran a narrow smoke suite covering the 5 critical backend endpoints + health, and verified the login page renders in Indonesian by default with a working ID/EN switcher. All 6 backend smoke tests pass; frontend login page renders cleanly with no console errors. Gitignore fix is inert as expected.

gitignore_verification.interpretation: Exit 1 from `git check-ignore` confirms file is NOT ignored. -v output shows the negation rule on line 86 is what was matched, overriding the .env.* glob block (line 84). Future `git add .env.example` will succeed.
gitignore_verification.note: File is currently untracked in the local working copy (not yet `git add`ed), which is expected — main agent / user must commit & push it.

frontend_smoke_results.default_language: Indonesian (Selamat datang kembali, Kata Sandi, Masuk)
frontend_smoke_results.after_EN_click: English UI confirmed (Welcome back, Password, Sign in)

action_items: None for code. Main agent / user should now `git add .env.example && git commit -m 'chore: include .env.example template' && git push` so the template ships to the VPS on next `git clone` / `git pull`.
critical_code_review_comments: Smoke test note: POST /api/api-keys schema requires `label` (not `name`). Worth documenting in API docs / OpenAPI examples for API consumers.
test_credentials: admin@wagateway.com / admin123 (from review request; matches login page demo credentials box)
seed_data_creation: None. Test API key and invitation created with TEST_ prefix; API key cleaned up in-test.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Iteration 6 was a .gitignore-only fix verification. Backend & frontend untouched. Iteration 5 baseline (58/61 backend, 9/9 invitation flow) still holds. New smoke file at /app/backend/tests/test_gitignore_smoke.py is reusable for quick regression. Next iterations can skip re-verifying the gitignore unless that file is touched again.
rca of the issue: Original RCA was correct: `.gitignore` line 84 `.env.*` glob excluded `.env.example` from `git add`, so VPS `git clone` never received the template. Fix added `!.env.example` (line 86) and `!.env.sample` (line 87) negation rules. Verified with `git check-ignore .env.example` returning exit 1 (= not ignored). Mitigation: main agent should `git add .env.example && git commit && git push` to deliver the template to the remote, then user's next `git pull` on the VPS will receive it.
